globalization.description: The process by which businesses develop international influence or start operating on an international scale.

porters_diamond.description: A model that explains how a nation's competitiveness is influenced by four attributes: factor conditions, demand conditions, related and supporting industries, and firm strategy, structure, and rivalry.

global_strategies.description: Plans developed by a company to achieve competitive advantage in different international markets, involving varying levels of standardization and adaptation.

entry_modes.description: Methods used by companies to enter foreign markets, including exporting, licensing, franchising, joint ventures, and wholly owned subsidiaries.

strategic_alliances.description: Collaborative agreements between firms to pursue a set of agreed-upon objectives while remaining independent organizations.
